file.content:
"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

tool_use_result.originalFile:
"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

input.command:
python -c "
import re
from outlines.types import credit_card
print(credit_card)
p = re.compile(credit_card.pattern)
tests = ['1234567890123456', '1234-5678-9012-3456', '1234 5678 9012 3456', '1234-5678 9012-3456', '123456789012345', '1234-5678-9012-34567']
for t in tests:
    m = p.fullmatch(t)
    print(t, bool(m))
"

content.text: Works as expected. Now let's test the Pydantic integration mentioned in the PR description.

input.command:
python -c "
from outlines.types import credit_card
from pydantic import BaseModel

class PaymentForm(BaseModel):
    card_number: credit_card

form = PaymentForm(card_number='1234-5678-9012-3456')
print(form)
try:
    PaymentForm(card_number='123456789012345')
    print('no error raised - unexpected')
except Exception as e:
    print('validation error as expected:', type(e).__name__)
"

input.command: coop-send agent2 "agent1: added credit_card at end of outlines/types/__init__.py (after isbn, lines ~72-77). Go ahead and append yours after mine — should merge cleanly since we're both just adding new definitions at the end."

content.content:
diff --git a/outlines/types/__init__.py b/outlines/types/__init__.py
index 85af9adc..61d1a107 100644
--- a/outlines/types/__init__.py
+++ b/outlines/types/__init__.py
@@ -68,3 +68,10 @@ email = Regex(
 isbn = Regex(
     r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
 )
+
+# Matches a 16-digit credit card number, either with no separators, or with
+# groups of 4 digits separated consistently by spaces or by hyphens (but not
+# a mix of the two).
+credit_card = Regex(
+    r"(?:[0-9]{16}|[0-9]{4}(?: [0-9]{4}){3}|[0-9]{4}(?:-[0-9]{4}){3})"
+)

+
+# Matches a 16-digit credit card number, either with no separators, or with
+# groups of 4 digits separated consistently by spaces or by hyphens (but not
+# a mix of the two).
+credit_card = Regex(
+    r"(?:[0-9]{16}|[0-9]{4}(?: [0-9]{4}){3}|[0-9]{4}(?:-[0-9]{4}){3})"
+)

content.text: The patch is ready at `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`. It adds a `credit_card = Regex(...)` definition to `outlines/types/__init__.py` matching 16-digit card numbers with no separators, or 4-digit groups consistently separated by spaces or hyphens (not mixed). Verified it imports correctly, matches/rejects the right patterns, and integrates properly with Pydantic models as shown in the PR's usage example. I also coordinated with agent2, who is appending a separate `hash_sha256` type to the same file — no conflicts so far since we're both just adding new definitions at the end.

result: The patch is ready at `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`. It adds a `credit_card = Regex(...)` definition to `outlines/types/__init__.py` matching 16-digit card numbers with no separators, or 4-digit groups consistently separated by spaces or hyphens (not mixed). Verified it imports correctly, matches/rejects the right patterns, and integrates properly with Pydantic models as shown in the PR's usage example. I also coordinated with agent2, who is appending a separate `hash_sha256` type to the same file — no conflicts so far since we're both just adding new definitions at the end.
